question.text: Which of the following is NOT an assumption of Standard Finance theory?

answer.text: Investors are rational and self-interested utility maximizers
Investors update their beliefs when they receive new information
Investors are guided by psychological biases in their decision making
Investors have access to all available information

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Standard Finance assumes investors are rational, risk averse, self-interested utility maximizers who update beliefs with new information and have access to all information. The idea that biases guide investment decisions is a core tenet of Behavioral Finance, not Standard Finance. **The Trap:** "Update beliefs with new information" may sound behavioral, but it is actually a Standard Finance assumption — rational agents process all new data efficiently. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A (Investment Adviser Level 1) also tests the efficient market hypothesis, which is built on Standard Finance assumptions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Understanding the gap between Standard and Behavioral Finance helps advisers recognize why clients don't always act "rationally" — this awareness is the first step to better advice.
